Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru {{{{Infobox ancient site| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale burial mounds in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 burial mounds, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale burial mounds in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 burial mounds, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale burial mounds in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 burial mounds, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 burial mounds, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale burial mounds in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 burial mounds, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale burial mounds in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 burial mounds, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale burial mounds in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 burial mounds, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] {{Infobox ancient site| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale burial mounds in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 burial mounds, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale burial mounds in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 burial mounds, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale burial mounds in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 burial mounds, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] {{Infobox ancient site| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] {{Infobox ancient site| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == {{Infobox ancient site| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]]
