While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| Scale=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| Construction Period=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest scale in Kyushu region| Map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]][[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]][[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]][[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]][[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Mezasahozuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mezasahozuka Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mezasahozuka Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest scale Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-scale keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the scale and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in scale.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] revision.comment: another mass replace to make the parameters work correctly
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| Latitude_deg=32|Latitude_min=7|Latitude_sec=11.78| Longitude_deg=131|Longitude_min=23|Longitude_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| map_size=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| coordinates_deg=32|coordinates_min=7|coordinates_sec=11.78| coordinates_deg=131|coordinates_min=23|coordinates_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| Shape={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| map_size=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| Special Notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun part_of|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun part_of|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun part_of|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun part_of|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun part_of exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun part_of|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| coordinates_deg=32|coordinates_min=7|coordinates_sec=11.78| coordinates_deg=131|coordinates_min=23|coordinates_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| type={{ill|Keyhole-typed tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| map_size=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[5th century]]| Burial Facility=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-typed tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-typed tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == Front view of the anterior portion| Alias= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| coordinates_deg=32|coordinates_min=7|coordinates_sec=11.78| coordinates_deg=131|coordinates_min=23|coordinates_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| map_size=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| alternate_name= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| coordinates_deg=32|coordinates_min=7|coordinates_sec=11.78| coordinates_deg=131|coordinates_min=23|coordinates_sec=4.76| ISO=JP-45| type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| map_size=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| Artifacts={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designated "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designated it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. Artifacts including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designation1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| alternate_name= | Group={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}} Oaza Miyake| coordinates=32|coordinates=7|coordinates=11.78| coordinates=131|coordinates=23|coordinates=4.76| omitted=JP-45| type={{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}| map_size=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-designation1 "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has designation1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus forms part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Burial Candidate=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}| burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-other_designation1 "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|map_dot_label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|ja|宮崎県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} has other_designation1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地}} ({{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|ja|西都市}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Group|ja|西都原古墳群}} * {{ill|Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ}} [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
