Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]], and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|qq=}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]] [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]]

Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Interred_individual=(Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]| burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-Historic Site1 "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|map_dot_label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]], and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|qq=}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]] [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:宮崎県の古墳]]

Front view of the anterior portion| alternate_name= |part_of=[[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]] Oaza Miyake| coordinates=32|coordinates=7|coordinates=11.78| coordinates=131|coordinates=23|coordinates=4.76| omitted=JP-45| type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| map_size=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Interred_individual=(Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]| burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-Historic Site1 "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|map_dot_label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]], and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|qq=}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]] [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:宮崎県の古墳]] [[Category:西都市の歴史]] [[Category:Candidate Imperial Mausoleums]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| alternate_name= |part_of=[[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]] Oaza Miyake| coordinates=32|coordinates=7|coordinates=11.78| coordinates=131|coordinates=23|coordinates=4.76| omitted=JP-45| type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| map_size=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Interred_individual=(Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]| burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-Historic Site1 "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|map_dot_label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]], and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|qq=}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]] [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:西都市の歴史]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| alternate_name= |part_of=[[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]] Oaza Miyake| coordinates=32|coordinates=7|coordinates=11.78| coordinates=131|coordinates=23|coordinates=4.76| omitted=JP-45| type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| map_size=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Interred_individual=(Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]| burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-Historic Site1 "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|map_dot_label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]], and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]] [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}}

Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]] [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| alternate_name= |part_of=[[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[Miyazaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]] Oaza Miyake| coordinates=32|coordinates=7|coordinates=11.78| coordinates=131|coordinates=23|coordinates=4.76| omitted=JP-45| type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| map_size=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Interred_individual=(Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]| burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-Historic Site1 "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|map_dot_label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]], and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]] [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}}

The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]] [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=[[File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG|280px]]
Front view of the anterior portion| alternate_name= |part_of=[[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup)| location=[[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]] Oaza Miyake| coordinates=32|coordinates=7|coordinates=11.78| coordinates=131|coordinates=23|coordinates=4.76| omitted=JP-45| type=[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| map_size=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Interred_individual=(Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]| burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-Historic Site1 "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|map_dot_label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} }} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]], and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Group]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]] [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}}

Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]] [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}}

Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} | architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]], and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=|1=Miyazaki Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454180}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Mezasahozuka Kofun]] [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}}

Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Interred_individual=(Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]| burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-Historic Site1 "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|map_dot_label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} | architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]], and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=|1=Miyazaki Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454180}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11446520}}

Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Interred_individual=(Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]| burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-Historic Site1 "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|map_dot_label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} | architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]], and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|qq=|1=Miyazaki Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454180}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11446520}}

It is part of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]], and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|1=Miyazaki Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454180}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11446520}}

Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]]| burial=Unknown| Interred_individual=(Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]]| burial goods=[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-Historic Site1 "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|map_dot_label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} | architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], [[:en:Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]], and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]], which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|1=Miyazaki Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454180}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11446520}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * [[:en:Saito, Miyazaki|Saito, Miyazaki]], Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|1=Miyazaki Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454180}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * [[:en:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|Saitobaru Kofun Group]] * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya-hime]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11446520}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11446520}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
