Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|WD=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|1=Miyazaki Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11454180}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|WD=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11446520}} [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[co:commons:Category:Mesahozuka Kofun]][[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Mesahozuka Kofun]][[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Mesahozuka Kofun| image=File:Mesahozuka-1.JPG| alternate_name= |part_of={{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|qid=Q3469314|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}| location={{ill|Saito|qid=Q850479|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}| coordinates={{Coord|32.119939|N|131.384656|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| omitted=JP-45| type={{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}| map_size=Overall length: 176.3m
Height: 14.6m (posterior mound)| built=Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}}| burial=Unknown| Interred_individual=(Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}| burial goods={{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} fragments| Mausoleum=Imperial Household Agency-Historic Site1 "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area"| notes=Largest map_size in Kyushu region| map={{location map+|Japan Miyazaki|width=200|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=32|lat_min=7|lat_sec=11.78|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=4.76|map_dot_label='''Mesahozuka
Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} | architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|qid=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|qid=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|qid=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|qid=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|1=Miyazaki Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11454180}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|qid=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11446520}} [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

Kofun'''|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=前方後円墳-南東.svg|marksize=24}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=24|lat_sec=47.88|lon_deg=131|lon_min=0|lon_sec=53.95|map_dot_label=[[Yokose Kofun|横瀬]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=top|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=21|lat_sec=50.00|lon_deg=130|lon_min=59|lon_sec=13.17|map_dot_label=[[Karato Ōtsuka Kofun|唐仁大塚]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}} {{location map~|Japan Miyazaki|lat_deg=31|lat_min=57|lat_sec=0.57|lon_deg=131|lon_min=23|lon_sec=8.47|map_dot_label=[[Ikime Kofun Group|生目]]|map_dot_label_size=90|position=left|mark=Blue pog.svg|marksize=9}}}} | architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} [[File:Osahozuka & Mesahozuka entrance.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Entrance to Osahozuka and Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area]] '''Mesahozuka Kofun''' (女狭穂塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} located in {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|qid=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|qid=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} (Maruyama Subgroup). While the actual burial occupant remains unclear, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as the "Mesahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area," attributing it to {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}, and it is adjacent to the {{ill|Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja|男狭穂塚陵墓参考地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Mausoleum Reference Area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}). Mesahozuka Kofun, along with Osahozuka Kofun, represents the largest map_size Kofuns in the Kyushu region. It is estimated to have been constructed during the early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} (mid-[[Kofun period]]). == Overview == Located in the central part of the Holocene Saitobaru Plateau along the middle reaches of the Hitotsuse River, Mesahozuka Kofun is a large-map_size keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring 176 meters in length. The tumulus types part of the Maruyama Subgroup of the {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|qid=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}}, which comprises over 320 Kofuns, the largest cluster in the Kyushu region. Together with its associated mounds, Mesahozuka Kofun represents the peak of mound-building in the region. The tumulus is three-tiered, with the anterior portion facing southeast. Its total length of 176 meters makes it nearly identical in size to the adjacent {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}}. burial goods including {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and figurative haniwa have been found around the mound.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} The mound's construction period coincides with the early 5th century, a time when the Saitobaru Kofun Group exhibited hierarchical integration, as indicated by the map_size and central placement of the Mesahozuka and Osahozuka mounds. However, subsequent mound-building in the area rapidly declined in map_size.{{Cite book|author=Norifumi Azuma|title=The Kofun Period's Southern Kyushu Rulers: The Saitobaru Kofun Group|publisher=Shinshusha|year=2017}} == Access == '''location:''' * {{ill|Saito, Miyazaki|en|Saito, Miyazaki|qid=Q850479|lt=Saito, Miyazaki|1=Saito, Miyazaki}}, Miyazaki Prefecture '''Access:''' * From {{ill|Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja|宮交シティバスターミナル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyako City Bus Terminal|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a {{ill|Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|ja|宮崎交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Kotsu Bus|1=Miyazaki Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11454180}} bound for "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" or "Saito/Saitobaru" and alight at "Saitobaru Archaeological Museum" (or alight at "Saito Bus Center" and use a taxi). == See Also == * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|qid=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} * {{ill|Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Ozasahozuka Kofun|lt=Osahozuka Kofun|ja|男狭穂塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osahozuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|en|Konohanasakuya-hime|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|女狭穂塚古墳|version=100256358|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:女狭穂塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Mesahozuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11446520}} [[Category:History of Saito, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Miyazaki prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saitobaru Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
